description_long: This is a flat, irregularly shaped stone slab with a roughly circular outline and a slightly undulating surface. The stone displays a deep reddish-brown coloration with subtle variations in tone, suggesting natural mineral deposits. Its edges are uneven and somewhat jagged, giving it a rugged, unrefined appearance typical of quarried or naturally weathered stones. The surface is matte and rough, with visible textural details such as small pits and shallow grooves. There is a small area of handwritten markings or catalog numbers on one side, indicating it may have been collected or studied. The overall form is thin and plate-like, making it suitable as a ground-level obstacle or barrier in an urban setting.
description: A flat, irregular reddish-brown stone slab with rough, jagged edges and a matte, textured surface. It features subtle mineral color variations and visible catalog markings.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and flat, with its full surface and catalog markings visible, highlighting its irregular outline and rough texture.
description_view_1: Seen edge-on, the stone looks very thin and plate-like, emphasizing its minimal thickness and uneven perimeter.
description_view_2: The underside of the stone is visible, showing a darker, more shadowed surface with less detail and a pronounced curvature.
description_view_3: This angled view reveals the stone's thickness and jagged edge profile, as well as the location of the catalog markings near one corner.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and mineral streaks with no regular pattern; handwritten catalog markings on one side.
attribute_weathering: Rough and matte with minor pitting, indicating moderate natural weathering and exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or grassy urban surfaces but could blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
